papers_mined.title: Trans-Saharan trade (Wikipedia)
papers_mined.note: Caravan trade between North Africa and sub-Saharan Africa peaked 8th-17th centuries; oases + zango stations served as critical relay/stop points where information + goods accumulated.

papers_mined.title: Trans-Saharan Trade Sahel Damergu 1870-1930
papers_mined.note: North African merchants migrated to Damergu from Ghadames or diaspora; trade-station Hausa towns Katsina + Damergu accumulated news + goods from multiple caravans.

papers_mined.title: Trans-Saharan Caravan Trade Encyclopedia
papers_mined.note: Caravans organized in cooler months Oct-March; oases (zango) as relay stops where caravan information + commodity prices propagated.

papers_mined.title: Organization of Caravan Trade Cambridge
papers_mined.note: Caravans rented camels from pastoralists; zango = lodging/trade station where information cascade between caravans occurred.

papers_mined.title: CHAP Context Handoff Long-Running Penetration Testing NDSS 2026
papers_mined.note: LLM-side: CHAP context handoff agents in shifts passing compressed session states; +9.1 percentage-point improvement + 32.4% token reduction.

papers_mined.title: Context Folding Scaling Long-Horizon Agent OpenReview
papers_mined.note: LLM-side: Context Folding procedurally branches into sub-trajectories then folds + retains summary; 10x smaller active context.

papers_mined.title: Which LLM Multi-Agent Protocol Choose (2510.17149)
papers_mined.note: LLM-side: comparison of multi-agent protocols including cascade and serial-handoff patterns.

papers_mined.title: Chain of Agents NeurIPS 2024
papers_mined.note: LLM-side: chain-of-agents collaborates on long-context tasks via serial agent handoff with information cascade.
